header.title: How it works — full data flow

sections.title: App Router page
sections.summary: The default landing page — fetches notes server-side and renders the list.

annotations.1: Async Server Component — runs on the server, never ships to the client.
annotations.5: Direct DB call: no fetch, no API route, no extra hop.
annotations.12: Suspense boundary lets the static shell render while notes stream in.

sections.title: Root layout
sections.summary: Provides the HTML shell, font, and ThemeProvider. Runs once per session, not per route change.

annotations.4: metadata export feeds Next.js's SEO defaults — no <Head> needed.
annotations.10: Inter font hosted by Next — preloaded with display:swap to avoid FOIT.

sections.title: Note query
sections.summary: Drizzle ORM call wrapped in a memoised function so the layout and the page can both call it without double-querying.

annotations.1: React's cache() ensures a single DB call per request — even across components.
annotations.11: limit 50 — avoids unbounded payloads on accounts with thousands of notes.

sections.title: Create note action
sections.summary: Server action invoked from the client form. Validates with Zod, writes via Drizzle, revalidates the cache.

annotations.1: 'use server' marks this file as server-only code reachable from the client by RPC.
annotations.5: Zod parsing throws on invalid input — Next.js converts the throw into a structured form error.
annotations.14: revalidatePath flushes the cached page so the new note appears immediately.
